---
title: "My CV"
subtitle: "Kyle Cahitas"
date: today
toc: false
---

[Download PDF version](assets/docs/cv.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}

Environmental data scientist and graduate researcher, San Luis Obispo,
California.
[kcahitas@calpoly.edu](mailto:kcahitas@calpoly.edu) ·
[GitHub](https://github.com/Yahitas) ·
[LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/kylecahitas)

## Education

**M.S., Environmental Science and Management** — California Polytechnic State
University, San Luis Obispo. Expected July 2026. Cumulative GPA 3.78.

**B.S., Environmental Studies** — University of California, Santa Barbara. 2021
to 2024. GPA 3.61, Dean's Honors.

**Study Abroad (UCEAP), Environmental Science and Field Methods** — University
of Waikato, Hamilton, New Zealand. 2023.

## Research

**Master's thesis.** Structural predictors of end-of-life agricultural-plastic
disposal among California strawberry growers. Latent class analysis (poLCA in R)
of a statewide mail survey. Defended July 2026.

## Experience

**Graduate Researcher** — Cal Poly SLO. September 2024 to July 2026.

- Designed and ran latent class analysis (poLCA in R) on a statewide grower survey.
- Built a reproducible R pipeline: data cleaning, model selection, multiple-comparison correction.
- Built a numbers-ledger system locking every reported statistic to its source script.

**Teaching Assistant, ESCI 502 (R Coding Laboratory)** — Cal Poly SLO. January
to March 2026.

- Led weekly labs on R for statistical analysis and environmental data visualization.
- Coached students through tidyverse debugging and reproducible workflows.

**GIS Consultant** — California Strawberry Commission, Watsonville, CA. July to
December 2025.

- Built an ArcGIS Pro workflow for a life cycle assessment of agricultural plastics across five California counties.
- Ran closest-facility network analysis on field-to-facility haul distances.
- Interviewed CalRecycle managers and visited recycling centers.

**Training-Efficiency Analyst (Consultant)** — CAL FIRE, California. February
2025.

- Analyzed CAL FIRE training modules using participant survey feedback and summarized results with improvement recommendations.

**Capstone Field Researcher** — University of Waikato, New Zealand. July to
September 2023.

- Collected and georeferenced vegetation data and ran water-quality assessments at five sites.
- Ran transect-based spatial analysis of dune erosion across three monitoring sites.

**Field Assistant** — Your Children's Trees, UC Santa Barbara, Goleta, CA. July
to September 2022.

- Monitored 60 coast live oaks (*Quercus agrifolia*) at a restoration site and documented establishment success.
- Developed educational materials for local K-12 faculty.

**Field Research Assistant** — Storke Ranch Vernal Pools, Goleta, CA. September
to December 2021.

- Supported restoration of a sensitive vernal-pool ecosystem: invasive removal by weeding, solarization, and machinery.
- Seeded over 150 purple needlegrass (*Stipa pulchra*) to increase native biodiversity.

**Shop Manager** — The Loft Hawaiian Restaurant, Torrance, CA. December 2017 to
December 2021.

- Managed daily operations and staffing for a 20-person front-of-house team across peak service periods.

## Projects

**Knowledge-distillation random forest for shrub detection** — Carrizo Plain,
CA. Led the machine learning work on a teacher-student model that detects target
shrubs from drone RGB imagery for restoration monitoring. Client: Sequoia
Riverlands Trust.

**Pour-point identification from a watershed DEM** — San Luis Obispo, CA.
Compared geomorphon-based and D8 flow-accumulation methods for locating a
watershed outlet from a 10-meter elevation model, in Python. Poster, Cal Poly
CAFES Spring Research Symposium, 2026.

**Cultural-resources predictive sensitivity model** — SLO County. Methodology
writeup combining NHD hydrography, USGS 3DEP elevation, SSURGO soils, and
CalVeg, with weighted-overlay and machine learning options and spatially blocked
cross-validation.

## Technical skills

**R**: tidyverse, sf, terra, glm, poLCA, ggplot2.
**Python**: rasterio, geopandas, scikit-learn, pandas, numpy.
**GIS**: ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, weighted-overlay and multi-criteria decision
analysis, network analysis, watershed and pour-point analysis, spatial
cross-validation.
**Statistics and ML**: logistic regression, latent class analysis, random
forests, knowledge distillation, model selection and diagnostics, RAG pipelines,
local LLM deployment (Ollama).
**Field methods**: vegetation surveys, water-quality sampling, transect-based
erosion monitoring.
**Software**: RStudio, VS Code, Git and GitHub, Excel, Google Workspace, Obsidian.

## Presentations

Cahitas, K., and Williams, N. (2026). Beyond Beliefs: how structural factors
shape agricultural-plastics end-of-life management among California strawberry
growers. Oral presentation, AFHVS/ASFS Annual Meeting, Burlington, VT.

Cahitas, K., and Williams, N. (2026). Beyond Beliefs. Poster, IASNR Conference,
Corvallis, OR.

Cahitas, K., and Williams, N. (2026). Beyond Beliefs. Poster, Society for
Applied Anthropology Annual Meeting, Albuquerque, NM.

<!-- [VERIFY] before shipping: (1) email choice — calpoly used here; gmail also
on resumes. (2) GPA 3.78 / 3.61 and Dean's Honors are from the federal-master
resume only. (3) "Expected July 2026" — conferral date not yet posted. (4) shrub
project species/numbers conflict (see research/shrub-detection.qmd). Extra roles
on the federal resume (CAL FIRE, UCSB field roles, restaurant shop manager) are
now all included per your call. -->
